reasoning: The 10-year real interest rate estimate from the Cleveland Fed has been in a general downward trend over the past year, falling from approximately 1.96% in November 2024 to 1.61% in November 2025. Although there was a slight uptick in the most recent November data point (from 1.55% to 1.61%), market sentiment and expert forecasts as of November 2025 (e.g., UBS) suggest that Treasury yields are expected to resume their decline through early 2026 due to anticipated Federal Reserve rate cuts. The FRED statistical analysis indicates a 30% probability of an increase by December 2025 based on the linear trend and recent volatility. I have anchored on this value, slightly adjusted for the recent uptick momentum, and regressed toward 0.5 for the February 2026 date to account for increased uncertainty over a longer horizon.
